content.problemStatement:
You're given an array AAA of size NNN.

There is a complete weighted graph with NNN nodes and N‚ãÖ(N‚àí1)2\frac{N \cdot (N-1)}{2}2N‚ãÖ(N‚àí1)‚Äã undirected weighted edges.

The weight of edge between node iii and node jjj is determined as MEX(Ai,Ai+1,‚Ä¶,Aj)\text{MEX}(A_i, A_{i+1}, \ldots, A_j)MEX(Ai‚Äã,Ai+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Aj‚Äã).

Your task is to find the shortest path from node 111 to node NNN.

The MEX (Minimum Excluded Value) of a sequence is the smallest non-negative integer that is not present in the sequence. For example, the Mex of (5,2,2,0,1,6)(5, 2, 2, 0, 1, 6)(5,2,2,0,1,6) is 333, the Mex of (6,3,2,2,4)(6, 3, 2, 2, 4)(6,3,2,2,4) is 000, and the Mex of (6,0,3,2,1)(6, 0, 3, 2, 1)(6,0,3,2,1) is 444.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the array AAA;
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the shortest path from node 111 to node NNN.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The shortest path to move from node 111 to node 333 has length 222:

Move from node 111 to node 222 with cost MEX(A1,A2)=MEX(0,1)=2\text{MEX}(A_1,A_2)=\text{MEX}(0,1)=2MEX(A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã)=MEX(0,1)=2;
Move from node 222 to node 333 with cost MEX(A2,A3)=MEX(1,2)=0\text{MEX}(A_2,A_3)=\text{MEX}(1,2)=0MEX(A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã)=MEX(1,2)=0.

The total cost is 2+0=22+0=22+0=2.
Test case 222: You can move from node 111 to 333 with cost MEX(A1,A2,A3)=MEX(1,0,3)=2\text{MEX}(A_1,A_2,A_3)=\text{MEX}(1,0,3)=2MEX(A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,A3‚Äã)=MEX(1,0,3)=2.
It can be proven 222 is the minimum length of path.
